title: Intent Cords
desc: The living gate at the east path has to be calmed before visitors arrive.

steps.text:
The east gate of the Forest of Life is not a gate in the ordinary sense. It is a trellis of living vine, thornless but deliberate, grown over stone uprights and allowed to choose for itself how open it feels on a given day. When travelers arrive calm, it parts like lace. When they arrive angry, proud, or frightened enough to lash outward, it knots shut and makes everyone wait.

This morning the trellis has not exactly closed, but it has become difficult. The openings are narrow, the leaves are turned inward, and a cluster of pale flowers has folded itself away from the path. An OverWorld keeper sets down a wash basin and a packet of scented cords. "Too much tension on the road before sunrise," she says. "We remind the gate that not every arrival is a threat."

She offers you the two usual tasks. The stone at the entry needs rinsing until the runoff runs clear, or the scent cords need to be retied through the upper weave where the trellis reads the mood of the approach.

options.label: Wash the entry stones and basin lips
Retie the calming cords through the upper weave

steps.text:
You kneel by the threshold stones with a brush and cool water, scrubbing away the fine grit left by early traffic. It is slow work because the keeper is right: the gate notices more than dirt. You have to keep your movements measured, your breathing even, your hands free of the hurried frustration that rough work brings out.

At first nothing changes. Then the basin water clears, and one of the heavy side vines loosens by a finger's width. Another leaf uncurls enough to show its silver underside. The keeper does not praise the gate or command it. She only sets a clean hand on the nearest upright and lets the quiet lengthen.

By the time the first patrol arrives, the path has widened to something gracious again. The travelers do not even notice what almost kept them out. They pass through with lowered voices, and the gate remains open behind them.

options.label: Leave the threshold clean and calm

steps.text:
The scented cords are dyed soft yellow and pale green, each rubbed with a different oil before dawn. One smells faintly of mint. Another of warm bark after rain. Another carries almost no scent at all, only the clean absence of anything alarming. The keeper passes them up while you work them through the upper lattice where leaves and tendrils overlap.

The trellis reacts in tiny ways at first. A flower opens on the far side where you cannot quite reach it. A tendril that had curled hard around the upright eases its grip. When the final cord is tied, the whole arch seems to exhale. Sunlight reaches farther down the path. You can suddenly see the next turn without peering.

"Good," the keeper says quietly. "It was in no mood for boasting today." She runs a thumb along one of the cords and smiles when the vine beside it does not pull away. Sometimes, in the Forest of Life, success looks like being tolerated with increasing warmth.

options.label: Step back and let the gate accept the day
